description: A square designed graphic from the DHS 'Know2Protect' 'Top 10 Tips2Protect' online-child-safety series. Blue gradient background (light blue top, navy below) with cut-paper summer clip-art across the top: clouds, butterflies, a camera, a red-and-white life ring, a green popsicle, blue sunglasses, a watermelon slice, and a sun. A yellow rounded banner with black bold text reads 'TOP 10 TIPS2PROTECT.' Large white bold text at left reads 'TIP 5.' Center features an orange silhouette map of the contiguous United States with a magenta/pink map-pin location marker rising from it; the pin's circular center shows a black 'X' (an X-mark / the X logo style), symbolizing turning off location sharing. Large white centered bold text reads: 'Turn off location data services on social media and nonessential apps.' At bottom are the white DHS seal and the 'know2protect' speech-bubble logo with tagline 'Together We Can Stop Online Child Exploitation.' A parental-guidance public-safety awareness graphic about protecting children's location privacy online; target audience is parents and guardians.
notable_text: TOP 10 TIPS2PROTECT; TIP 5; Turn off location data services on social media and nonessential apps.; know2protect - Together We Can Stop Online Child Exploitation
